title: erysimum pulchellum subsp. sieheanum
intro: Erysimum pulchellum subsp. sieheanum, also known as Siehe's wallflower, is a subspecies of flowering plant in the family Brassicaceae. It is a perennial plant with fragrant yellow flowers and has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties. The plant contains compounds such as isothiocyanates, which have been shown to possess anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, potentially providing health benefits such as reducing inflammation and fighting off infections. Erysimum pulchellum subsp. sieheanum can be prepared as a herbal tea or tincture, although its use should be approached with caution and under the guidance of a medical professional due to potential interactions with other medications.
